Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are provided with a normative process description and an event log We need to list cases that deviate from intended process flow as well as explanation of why these deviations violate the normative rules

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The text describes a normative process for handling orders It says the general idea is that an order once received should be handled in accordance with availability Process always begins with Receive order After receiving next logical step is to check inventory Check inventory Then if inventory is sufficient sequence Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order If insufficient then branch into cancellation scenario after checking inventory process directly proceeds to Cancel order and then Close order

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Normative description states the constraints Authorize payment should occur after Check inventory and before packaging Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping Ship order should occur after packaging and never after cancellation Close order is final activity in all scenarios

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 This case is exactly matching intended sequence for sufficient inventory Receive check authorize package ship close So it is correct

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2 Here we see violation Package items is executed before Authorize payment According to normative process Authorization should occur after check inventory and before packaging So in case 2 the sequence is wrong because it is doing package items then authorize payment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3 The description says if inventory insufficient after check inventory the process should directly proceed to Cancel order so no authorization payment is allowed in cancellation branch But here case 3 has both Authorize payment and then Cancel order Also normative text says Ship order should never occur after a cancellation In this event log after cancelling there is shipping

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 This is valid for cancellation scenario as described After checking inventory directly cancel order and then close order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 5 We see violation Package items is missing The normative description says once payment is authorized the items should be prepared for shipment and only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order So in case 5 shipping order was executed without packaging

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus summary Case 2 violates the sequence ordering by executing Package items before Authorize payment Case 3 has multiple deviations it authorizes payment in a cancellation branch and then shipping order is performed after cancellation Case 5 violates the sequence by skipping the Package items step Case 1 and case 4 are correct sequences as per normative process
